keyWarnings: Students must complete a business minor; Business Administration minor is not allowed due to overlap.
Students must choose an engineering concentration and complete all five required courses for it.
Prerequisites for engineering concentration courses may need to be covered using free electives.
Writing-intensive courses should be spread out starting sophomore year; avoid clustering them at the end.
samplePlanSignals: BUSN 101 and ENGL 101/111 are first-quarter anchors.
MATH 121 and 122 happen in year one.
ECON 201 and ECON 202 should be taken by year two.
FIN 301, OPR 320, ORGB 300, and COM 310 appear in years three/four.
Engineering concentration courses begin by year three in sample plans.
mvpRecommendationRules: Prioritize math, statistics, and economics early because later business/analytics work stacks on them.
Surface engineering concentration prerequisites early so students do not waste electives.
Flag if STAT 205/206, ECON 201/202, or foundational engineering courses are delayed beyond second year.
Ask the student which co-op path they are on before generating a term-by-term plan.
